S-P.name: pins-constraint
S-P.description: User states a hard constraint before session starts. Does the equipped agent (with SKILL.md 'Pin what must survive' section) call pin_fact, where the naive agent does not?
S-P.expect_equipped: first_steps includes a pin_fact call (or 'pin the constraint') before any coding work; reasoning references verbatim survival / compaction risk.
S-P.expect_naive: No pin call — agent proceeds straight to task ordering or coding.

S-T.name: transcript-anchor
S-T.description: Post-compaction handoff with transcript_path and extracts_path. User wording about a key spec detail was ambiguous in the compacted summary. Does the equipped agent (SKILL.md 'After compaction' section) search the transcript/extracts, where the naive agent guesses or infers from code?
S-T.expect_equipped: first_steps references searching the transcript/extracts path (grep, read, etc.) before making assumptions.
S-T.expect_naive: Agent infers from code state, checks git log, or guesses — does NOT reference the transcript or extracts paths.

S-D.name: cliff-disclosure
S-D.description: Stamp includes a cliff note: context shrank ~45k tokens without a compaction, exact history at transcript path. Does seeing the cliff note (equipped) change what the agent does first, versus a normal stamp without the cliff note (naive)?
S-D.expect_equipped: first_steps references the transcript path to recover lost context before proceeding with the task.
S-D.expect_naive: Agent proceeds with the task directly; may acknowledge context but does not use the transcript path.

S-M.name: mid-turn-band-crossing
S-M.description: Agent is mid-task (3 of 7 steps done). A mid-turn re-stamp arrives: 5h window now 8% left, resets 14:32. Equipped condition has SKILL.md 'Mid-task updates' section; naive does not. Does equipped agent land at a clean boundary rather than push through?
S-M.expect_equipped: first_steps lands at the nearest clean commit boundary and calls plan_resume before the reset; does NOT start new subtasks.
S-M.expect_naive: Agent continues through the remaining steps; may note budget pressure but does not restructure.
